19:
Hall Street Historic District

A nationally recognized historic area listed on the National Register of Historic Places since 1979. The neighborhood, sometimes called Millionaire's Row, was developed between 1870 and 1920 and reflects St. Joseph’s peak prosperity during the westward expansion era, when the city had the highest per-capita income in the U.S.

The district spans 19.4 acres (7.9 ha), and initially featured 43 contributing buildings that provided exceptional examples of Gilded Age architecture. It originally included Italianate, Second Empire, Chateauesque, Richardsonian Romanesque, and Queen Anne style homes. Although several structures have been lost to fire and neglect, many of the beautiful surviving homes have been preserved or restored and now serve as bed-and-breakfasts, apartments, or private residences.
